title: Argyranthemum pinnatifidum
intro: Argyranthemum pinnatifidum, also known as Canary Islands daisy or Marguerite, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions like arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Argyranthemum pinnatifidum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support digestive health and relieve menstrual discomfort.
taxonomy: Argyranthemum pinnatifidum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Argyranthemum, which is a group of evergreen shrubs native to the Canary Islands. The genus Argyranthemum is further classified under the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. Argyranthemum pinnatifidum is one of the many species within the Argyranthemum genus. It is closely related to other daisy family plants, which share similar characteristics and growth habits.
